The input requirement describes ventilator behavior in PCV and PSV modes, specifically regarding the Inspiratory Pause function triggered by the GUI. It states that when the user activates Inspiratory Pause (via the GUI button), after the next inspiratory phase completes (i.e., at inspiratoryPhaseEnd), if the pause is still requested, both the inspiratory and expiratory valves (inValveOpen and outValveOpen) should close (so inValveOpen = False and outValveOpen = False), and maintain this state until the GUI tells it to stop (inspiratoryPauseButton becomes false).

To capture this, the main conditions are:
1. The trigger: Inspiratory Pause is requested (inspiratoryPauseButton).
2. After the next inspiration phase ends (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), AND if the pause is still requested, both valves close (inValveOpen is False & outValveOpen is False), and remain closed until the user stops the Inspiratory Pause via GUI (i.e., inspiratoryPauseButton is false).
3. The entire behavior is only relevant in PCV and PSV modes (PCVMode | PSVMode).

The decomposition uses:
- decision1: The high-level context is while we are in PCV or PSV mode.
- decision2: upon Inspiratory Pause being activated (inspiratoryPauseButton).
- decision3: until Inspiratory Pause is deactivated (inspiratoryPauseButton is false), at the end of next inspiration (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), immediately both valves close (not inValveOpen & not outValveOpen).

This translation tightly matches the textual description: it gates the behavior to the two required modes, and aligns steps and timings (at inspiration end, effect holds until pause is released).

explanation: This translation captures the two relevant ventilation modes (PCVMode and PSVMode), and the condition that the Inspiratory Pause can be initiated if set by the GUI (inspiratoryPauseButton). When this is set, after the end of the next inspiration phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), if an inspiratory pause is still required (inspiratoryPauseButton & inspiratoryPause), then both the inspiratory valve (inValveOpen) and expiratory valve (outValveOpen) must remain closed (!inValveOpen & !outValveOpen) until the pause is stopped (i.e., inspiratoryPauseButton is no longer true). The structured NL captures that, in PCV and PSV modes, when the pause is requested by the GUI, the system should respond by closing the relevant valves at the correct moment and holding them closed until the command is lifted.

explanation: This translation captures the requirement that in PCV and PSV modes, if the inspiratory pause is set by the GUI, at the end of the next inspiratory phase, and as long as the pause is still required, both inspiratory and expiratory valves should close until the pause is explicitly stopped via the GUI. The translation first scopes the property to only PCV and PSV mode scenarios, then specifies that upon the inspiratory pause being set, after the end of the current inspiration phase, both valves must close as long as the inspiratory pause is wanted. Since the request is to act immediately after the next inspiration ends, the decision template using 'after 1 tick' is appropriate.
